---
title: "Solitary Man 2022"
startDate: "2022-06-19T00:00:00.000Z"
tripPlan: content/trip-plans/condorphamine-and-solitary-2022.mdx
destinations:
  - route: content/routes/solitary-man.mdx
---

After climbing Condor, we wanted to try out Solitary Man as a group of 3 with Josh and Alexis.

The route is NOT bolted for rappels (you're expected to rappel the other route, or walk off the gully). The route just has hangers, no rings/chains.

## P1 - 5.10-, 30m, ~9 bolts

Our info claimed this was 5.9, 35 meters, but it definitely felt 5.10-, and it definitely was just under or exactly at 30 meters. Pretty sustained at 5.9/5.10- for a number of moves, very slabby.

## P2 - 5.10, 27m, ~8 bolts

Our info claimed this was 5.10-, 30m, but it definitely felt like 5.10 slab at one crux move, and was definitely only 27m. Pretty sustained pitch.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2022/07/PXL_20220619_183811272.bcPCxRL-768x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:1,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;x&quot;:404,&quot;y&quot;:398,&quot;w&quot;:10,&quot;h&quot;:10,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:1,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;x&quot;:240,&quot;y&quot;:374,&quot;w&quot;:10,&quot;h&quot;:10,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:1,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;x&quot;:214,&quot;y&quot;:330,&quot;w&quot;:10,&quot;h&quot;:10,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0757142857142856,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:327,&quot;y&quot;:834},{&quot;x&quot;:329,&quot;y&quot;:606},{&quot;x&quot;:259,&quot;y&quot;:553},{&quot;x&quot;:251,&quot;y&quot;:476},{&quot;x&quot;:228,&quot;y&quot;:444},{&quot;x&quot;:209,&quot;y&quot;:431},{&quot;x&quot;:159,&quot;y&quot;:439},{&quot;x&quot;:112,&quot;y&quot;:430},{&quot;x&quot;:81,&quot;y&quot;:414},{&quot;x&quot;:135,&quot;y&quot;:351},{&quot;x&quot;:185,&quot;y&quot;:313},{&quot;x&quot;:248,&quot;y&quot;:267}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"

![Start of P2](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2022/07/PXL_20220619_183815421.nQca4wrH-1024x768.jpg)
![P2 from the anchor](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2022/07/PXL_20220619_185128970.RCFXYA9Z-1024x768.jpg)

## P3-5 - 5.6-8, ~90m

We linked pitches 3-5. I climbed almost to the top of P4 when we started simul'ing together. P3 felt 5.7, P4 was 5.8, and P5 was 5.6.

![Looking down P4](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2022/07/PXL_20220619_192952276.9edOfjE-225x300.jpg)

![Top!](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2022/07/PXL_20220619_195029532.BBxgMmXO-1024x768.jpg)
